targets.mechanism: Neuraminidase cleaves terminal sialic acids and supports viral release from infected cells.
targets.recommendation_reason: Best first closed-loop target: clinically validated, clear enzymatic pocket, public co-crystal structures, known inhibitors, and straightforward biochemical assay.
historical_or_resistance_notes: NA inhibitor resistance can occur, for example around H275Y in N1 numbering contexts.
Use current sequence and resistance surveillance if making a real-world claim.

representative_structures.description: 2009 pandemic H1N1 neuraminidase complexed with oseltamivir
2009 pandemic H1N1 neuraminidase complexed with zanamivir
2009 pandemic H1N1 neuraminidase complexed with laninamivir

binding_site.description: Conserved neuraminidase active site around the co-crystallized inhibitor, with charged and polar contacts that recognize sialic-acid-like ligands.
binding_site.box_strategy: Use co-crystallized oseltamivir centroid from 3TI6 or a prepared receptor-ligand complex.

generation_guidance.primary_goal: Generate drug-like neuraminidase active-site binders that retain key polar/charged recognition while exploring non-identical scaffolds.
must_have: hydrogen-bonding or ionic interaction handles for the NA active site
balanced polarity suitable for NA pocket docking
diverse analog families around known NA inhibitor interaction patterns
avoid: unreasonably large glycan-like molecules for first-pass docking
near-duplicate copies of known drugs unless used as controls
desired_properties: MW preferably below 550 for first-pass virtual screening
pose compatible with the conserved NA active site
generation_guidance.selectivity_notes: Prefer influenza neuraminidase active-site interactions over generic highly charged molecules that may score artificially.

computational: Dock generated molecules into 3TI6-derived NA active site.
Run PoseBusters on top docked poses.
Compare generated molecules against controls by rank, pose pass rate, and scaffold diversity.
experimental: Neuraminidase inhibition assay is the most direct biochemical validation route.
Cell-based influenza replication assays are downstream confirmation, not first-line CLI validation.

targets.mechanism: Influenza B neuraminidase supports viral release and is inhibited by approved neuraminidase inhibitors.
targets.recommendation_reason: Good target for B-flu workflows, but first MVP should start with A/H1N1 NA because the target story and demo structure set are simpler.
historical_or_resistance_notes: Influenza B susceptibility and resistance should be interpreted separately from influenza A.

representative_structures.description: Influenza B neuraminidase structure family example; verify ligand and chain before docking.

binding_site.description: Conserved neuraminidase active site; use a B-flu co-crystal structure when available.
binding_site.box_strategy: Use co-crystallized inhibitor centroid or validated NA active-site alignment.

generation_guidance.primary_goal: Generate B-flu neuraminidase candidates while respecting the conserved NA active-site recognition pattern.
must_have: polar interaction handles for NA active site
diverse scaffolds around known NA inhibitor patterns
avoid: blindly transferring A-flu residue numbering without structure alignment
overly charged molecules that are hard to rank with docking alone
desired_properties: pose compatible with B-flu NA active site

computational: Use known NA inhibitors as controls.
Verify the selected B-flu PDB and ligand before docking.
Run docking and pose checks as with A/H1N1 NA.
experimental: Neuraminidase inhibition assay with influenza B NA.
Cell-based follow-up only after biochemical enrichment.

targets.display_name: Influenza A polymerase acidic protein cap-dependent endonuclease
targets.mechanism: The PA endonuclease supports cap-snatching during viral transcription; baloxavir acid inhibits this activity.
targets.recommendation_reason: Clinically validated through baloxavir and attractive as an AI-drug-discovery target, but docking setup is metal-dependent and more complex than NA.
historical_or_resistance_notes: PA I38 substitutions are associated with reduced baloxavir susceptibility.
Metal coordination and tautomer/protonation state need careful preparation.

representative_structures.description: Influenza A/California/04/2009 PA endonuclease with bound baloxavir acid

binding_site.description: Metal-dependent PA endonuclease active site bound by baloxavir acid.
binding_site.box_strategy: Use baloxavir acid centroid and preserve catalytic metal treatment during receptor preparation.

generation_guidance.primary_goal: Generate docking-ready analogs that can occupy the PA endonuclease active site without overfitting to baloxavir.
avoid: claims of baloxavir resistance coverage without assay data
desired_properties: pose compatible with PA active site
generation_guidance.selectivity_notes: Screen for nonspecific metal chelation risk.

computational: Use baloxavir acid as positive control.
Treat docking results as lower confidence unless pose chemistry is reviewed.
experimental: Cap-dependent endonuclease inhibition or cell-based susceptibility assay.

targets.display_name: Influenza B polymerase acidic protein cap-dependent endonuclease
targets.mechanism: Influenza B PA endonuclease is targeted by baloxavir-class inhibition.
targets.recommendation_reason: Relevant for B-flu and clinically connected to baloxavir, but more complex than NA for first MVP.
historical_or_resistance_notes: Resistance interpretation should be subtype and assay specific.

representative_structures.description: Influenza B endonuclease with bound baloxavir acid

binding_site.description: Metal-dependent PA endonuclease active site bound by baloxavir acid.
binding_site.box_strategy: Use ligand centroid and preserve metal handling.

generation_guidance.primary_goal: Generate B-flu PA endonuclease candidates for computational screening.
desired_properties: pose compatible with PA active site

targets.mechanism: M2 is a proton channel involved in viral uncoating and historically targeted by adamantanes.
targets.recommendation_reason: Useful as a historical control, but adamantane resistance is widespread and CDC guidance does not recommend amantadine/rimantadine for currently circulating influenza A viruses.
historical_or_resistance_notes: Adamantanes are not active against influenza B.
Widespread resistance makes this a poor first target for a practical discovery demo.

representative_structures.description: Influenza A M2 transmembrane peptide bound to amantadine

binding_site.description: Membrane channel binding site; not a straightforward soluble enzyme pocket.
key_residues: M2 channel pore residues; verify strain and resistance mutation state
binding_site.box_strategy: Requires membrane/channel-aware preparation; not recommended for first docking MVP.

generation_guidance.primary_goal: Use as a negative or historical target-control example rather than a first discovery target.
avoid: presenting M2 as a recommended current influenza target
desired_properties: small channel-compatible molecules if used only as method comparison
generation_guidance.selectivity_notes: Not suitable as primary MVP target.

computational: Use only as historical control or method stress test.
Do not rank alongside NA without explicit caveats.
experimental: Channel inhibition and resistance-specific viral assays would be needed.
